evals.prompt: Decompose epic 'Multi-tenant support' (currently 21 points) for TaskFlow. Must include data isolation, per-tenant billing, per-tenant branding, admin tenant switcher. Break into stories that fit in a 2-week sprint.
evals.expected_output: 4-6 vertical stories each ≤8 points, created in Plane with parent set to the epic, total ≈21, each with user story format + acceptance criteria. Uses Split by Business Rule or Split by Workflow Step pattern.
expectations: Uses Split by Business Rule or Split by Workflow Step, NOT horizontal slicing by layer
Creates 4-6 child stories, each ≤ 8 points
Each child is independently deliverable (a tenant admin could use one without the others)
Child points sum to ~21 (preservation rule)
Uses User Story template with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria in description_html
Calls create_work_item with parent field set to the epic ID

evals.prompt: Split this 13-point story: 'User can manage their profile' — covers viewing, editing name/bio, uploading picture, and deleting account.
evals.expected_output: Uses Split by Workflow Step pattern, produces 3-4 children summing to ~13 points, each a vertical slice.
expectations: Recognizes this as Split by Workflow Step pattern
Creates 3-4 child stories (view / edit name+bio / upload picture / delete account)
Child points sum to ~13 (preservation rule)
Each child is a vertical slice (not split by FE/BE/DB layers)
Creates children in Plane with parent field pointing to the original

evals.prompt: Someone split 'Build signup flow' into: (1) Create users DB schema, (2) Build auth API endpoints, (3) Build signup UI. Review this decomposition.
evals.expected_output: Rejects — this is a horizontal (layer-based) slice. None of the three deliver value alone and all depend on each other. Recommend splitting by feature/behavior instead.
expectations: Identifies this as the horizontal slicing anti-pattern
Explains that none of the 3 pieces deliver value independently
Fails INVEST 'Independent' and 'Valuable' criteria
Suggests vertical alternative: signup with happy path → validation errors → email verification

evals.prompt: Validate this user story against INVEST: 'As a support agent, I want to flag tickets, so that I can follow up later' — 21 points, no acceptance criteria, depends on a new tags system being built first.
evals.expected_output: Fails Small (21 pts), Testable (no AC), and Independent (blocked by tags system). Specific remediation for each.
expectations: Checks each INVEST criterion individually
Reports Small: FAIL (21 pts > 8), split required
Reports Testable: FAIL (no acceptance criteria), write Given/When/Then
Reports Independent: FAIL (blocked by tags system), either wait or scope it in
Does NOT just say 'looks good'
